goal: Ask-a-Question draw board should fit one screen; Continue was overlapping the bottom nav
symptom: On a standard tall phone the question-draw board overflowed and the Continue button overlapped the fixed bottom-nav.
root_cause: question-draw's force-fit block was gated on @media (max-height:780px), which never matches a standard iPhone (~844-932 CSS px), so the one-screen compaction never applied on real phones (daily/full use max-width:640px).
fix: Replaced the max-height:780px block with @media (max-width:640px): the .question-draw-shell becomes a 100dvh flex column; the 3-card board flexes (flex:1, min-height:0, align-content:center, cards max 52px) to fill leftover height; Continue pinned to the bottom via margin-top:auto with padding-bottom clearing the bottom-nav + safe-area; footer hidden during selection.
verified: NOT visually QA'd — browser busy; needs a Dunkin check on a phone width
notes: CSS is shared by EN + th/question-draw.html (body[data-page] scoped), so no separate TH change. iOS css mirror is gitignored (on-disk for the build).
